sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of litchis grown from Litchi chinensis Sonn., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Litchis for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of litchis grown from Litchi chinensis Sonn., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Litchis for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties (cultivars) of litchis grown from Litchi chinensis Sonn., of the Sapindaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Litchis for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: Minimum Requirements
Classification
sections.text: Litchis are classified in three classes defined below:
sections.retrieval_text:

number=2.2

Litchis are classified in three classes defined below:

page_spans.text: Litchis are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: The litchis must be sufficiently developed and mature.
path_titles: The litchis must be sufficiently developed and mature.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the litchis must be such as to enable them:
- to withstand transport and handling; and
- to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.
The colouring of litchis may vary from pink to red in the case of untreated litchis; from pale yellow to pink for litchis that have been fumigated with sulphur dioxide.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Litchis in this class must be of superior quality. They must have the shape, development and colouring that are typical of the variety or varietal type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Litchis in this class must be of superior quality. They must have the shape, development and colouring that are typical of the variety or varietal type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Litchis in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety or varietal type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:
- slight misshaping;
- slight defects in colouring;
- slight skin defects provided these do not exceed a total area of 0.25 cm².

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: - Size is determined by the maximum equatorial diameter. - The minimum size for “Extra” Class is 33 mm. - The minimum size for Classes I and II is 20 mm. - A maximum size range of 10 mm between fruit in each package is permitted.

sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of litchis not satisfying the requirements as regards the minimum size, provided, however, that the diameter is not less that 15  mm in all classes, and/or the maximum size range of 10 mm.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only litchis of the same origin, variety or varietal type, quality, size and colour.  The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only litchis of the same origin, variety or varietal type, quality, size and colour.  The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Litchis must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Litchis shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).  However, the presence of a limited number of fresh leaves is permitted where litchis are presented in bunches.

page_spans.text: Litchis must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new2, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Litchis shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).  However, the presence of a limited number of fresh leaves is permitted where litchis are presented in bunches.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes litchis which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above.  The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the litchis retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:  - defects in shape; - defects in colouring; - skin blemishes provided these do not exceed a total area of 0.5 cm².

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the litchis. Packages (or lot for produce presented in bulk) must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

HYGIENE

sections.title: It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.

classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. classification.sentence: Country of origin and, optionally, district where grown or national, regional or local place name.
classification.pattern: [Main subject] and, optionally, [additional attributes or qualifiers] relating to place names.

components.optional_component: district where grown or national, regional or local place name

classification.justification: The sentence provides optional information attributes related to place names without imposing obligations, conditions, prohibitions, or limits, thus fitting a Permission modality with an informational communicative function. It does not match any known templates; its structure is a main subject with an optional extension listing place-related descriptors.
notes: The sentence is fragmentary and lacks a verb, which limits clarity on its exact communicative function. It appears to allow optional inclusion of certain details but does not explicitly state permission in a normative form.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR LITCHI
pages.summary: Página apresenta a continuação do padrão para lichia, com definição e provisões de qualidade detalhadas hierarquicamente. Estrutura clara de seções numeradas até 3 níveis, sem continuidade para próxima página.
Página apresenta continuação da seção 2.2.3 Class II com texto detalhado e completa seção 3 a 5 com subtítulos e texto explicativo organizado hierarquicamente, sem continuidade para página seguinte.

Página inicia com continuação da seção 5.2.1 e completa as subseções 5.3 e 6, além da seção 7, com diversos níveis de subseções numeradas e texto associado, sem indicações de continuidade para a próxima página.

pages.title: CXS 196-1995
pages.summary: Página contém uma seção 8 HYGIENE com duas subseções 8.1 e 8.2, ambas apresentando texto apenas em seus títulos sem texto interno adicional. A seção 8 já vinha da página anterior e não continua para as próximas páginas.
